alternateReportStorage.description: alternateReportStorage is the flag to enable alternate storage for all trivy reports (crds) in the form json files inside of a persistent volume

failEntriesLimit.description:
failEntriesLimit the flag to limit the number of fail entries per control check in the cluster compliance detail report
this limit is for preventing the report from being too large per control checks

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

name.description:
name specifies the name of the k8s Service Account. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

name.description:
name specifies the name of the k8s Service Account. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

imagePullSecret.description:
imagePullSecret is the secret name to be used when pulling trivy image from private registries example : reg-secret
It is the user responsibility to create the secret for the private registry in `trivy-operator` namespace

tag.description: tag version of the Trivy image

labels.description: labels is the extra labels to be used for trivy server statefulset

podLabels.description: podLabels is the extra pod labels to be used for trivy server

priorityClassName.description: priorityClassName is the name of the priority class used for trivy server

volumes.description:
- name: trusted-ca
mountPath: /etc/ssl/trusted-ca
subPath: trusted-ca.crt
readOnly: true
Statefulset volumes
- name: trusted-ca
secret:
secretName: trusted-ca

replicas.description: the number of replicas of the trivy-server

serverServiceName.description: serverServiceName this param is the server service name to be used in cluster

sslCertDir.description: sslCertDir can be used to override the system default locations for SSL certificate files directory, example: /ssl/certs

valuesFromConfigMap.description: vaulesFromConfigMap name of a ConfigMap to apply TRIVY_* environment variables. Will override Helm values.

valuesFromSecret.description: valuesFromSecret name of a Secret to apply TRIVY_* environment variables. Will override Helm AND ConfigMap values.

tag.description: tag version of the node-collector image

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

additionalVulnerabilityReportFields.description:
additionalVulnerabilityReportFields is a comma separated list of additional fields which
can be added to the VulnerabilityReport. Supported parameters: Description, Links, CVSS, Target, Class, PackagePath and PackageType

command.description:
command. One of `image`, `filesystem` or `rootfs` scanning, depending on the target type required for the scan.
For 'filesystem' and `rootfs` scanning, ensure that the `trivyOperator.scanJobPodTemplateContainerSecurityContext` is configured
to run as the root user (runAsUser = 0).

configFile.description:
configFile can be used to tell Trivy to use specific options available only in the config file (e.g. Mirror registries).
configFile:
registry:
mirrors:
index.docker.io:
- mirror.with.bad.auth
- mirror.without.image

dbRepositoryInsecure.description: The Flag to enable insecure connection for downloading trivy-db via proxy (air-gaped env)

filesystemScanCacheDir.description:
filesystemScanCacheDir the flag to set custom path for trivy filesystem scan `cache-dir` parameter.
Only applicable in filesystem scan mode.

ignoreFile.description:
ignoreFile can be used to tell Trivy to ignore vulnerabilities by ID (one per line)
ignoreFile:
- CVE-1970-0001
- CVE-1970-0002

ignoreFileName.description:
ignoreFileName sets the file name that will be mounted inside the scanner container.
Defaults to ".trivyignore" if not set.

ignoreUnfixed.description:
ignoreUnfixed is the flag to show only fixed vulnerabilities in
vulnerabilities reported by Trivy. Set to true to enable it.

imagePullSecret.description:
imagePullSecret is the secret name to be used when pulling trivy image from private registries example : reg-secret
It is the user responsibility to create the secret for the private registry in `trivy-operator` namespace

pullPolicy.description: pullPolicy is the imge pull policy used for trivy image , valid values are (Always, Never, IfNotPresent)

tag.description: tag version of the Trivy image

imageScanCacheDir.description:
imageScanCacheDir the flag to set custom path for trivy image scan `cache-dir` parameter.
Only applicable in image scan mode.

includeDevDeps.description:
includeDevDeps include development dependencies in the report (supported: npm, yarn) (default: false)
note: this flag is only applicable when trivy.command is set to filesystem

javaDbRegistry.description: javaDbRegistry is the registry for the Java vulnerability database.

offlineScan.description:
offlineScan is the flag to enable the offline scan functionality in Trivy
This will prevent outgoing HTTP requests, e.g. to search.maven.org

sbomSources.description: sbomSources trivy will try to retrieve SBOM from the specified sources (oci,rekor)

serverInsecure.description: serverInsecure is the flag to enable insecure connection to the Trivy server.

severity.description: severity is a comma separated list of severity levels reported by Trivy.

skipDirs.description: a comma separated list of directories for Trivy to skip

skipFiles.description: a comma separated list of file paths for Trivy to skip

skipJavaDBUpdate.description: skipJavaDBUpdate is the flag to enable skip Java index databases update for Trivy client.

slow.description: slow this flag is to use less CPU/memory for scanning though it takes more time than normal scanning. It fits small-footprint

sslCertDir.description: sslCertDir can be used to override the system default locations for SSL certificate files directory, example: /ssl/certs

supportedConfigAuditKinds.description: The Flag is the list of supported kinds separated by comma delimiter to be scanned by the config audit scanner

timeout.description: timeout is the duration to wait for scan completion.

useBuiltinRegoPolicies.description: The Flag to enable the usage of builtin rego policies by default, these policies are downloaded by default from mirror.gcr.io/aquasec/trivy-checks

useEmbeddedRegoPolicies.description:
To enable the usage of embedded rego policies, set the flag useEmbeddedRegoPolicies. This should serve as a fallback for air-gapped environments.
When useEmbeddedRegoPolicies is set to true, useBuiltinRegoPolicies should be set to false.

vulnType.description: vulnType can be used to tell Trivy to filter vulnerabilities by a pkg-type (library, os)

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

policiesConfig.description:
policiesConfig Custom Rego Policies to be used by the config audit scanner
See https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/blob/main/docs/tutorials/writing-custom-configuration-audit-policies.md for more details.

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

builtInServerRegistryInsecure.description: builtInServerRegistryInsecure is the flag to enable insecure connection from the built-in Trivy server to the registry.

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

debug.description: debug One of `true` or `false`. Enables debug mode.

serverServiceName.description: serverServiceName this param is the server service name to be used in cluster

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

clientServerSkipUpdate.description:
clientServerSkipUpdate is the flag to enable skip databases update for Trivy client.
Only applicable in ClientServer mode.

mode.description:
mode is the Trivy client mode. Either Standalone or ClientServer. Depending
on the active mode other settings might be applicable or required.

serverCustomHeaders.description:
serverCustomHeaders is a comma separated list of custom HTTP headers sent by
Trivy client to Trivy server. Only applicable in ClientServer mode.
serverCustomHeaders: "foo=bar"

serverToken.description:
serverToken is the token to authenticate Trivy client with Trivy server. Only
applicable in ClientServer mode.

serverPassword.description: serverPassword this param is the server user to be used to download db from private registry

serverToken.description:
serverToken is the token to authenticate Trivy client with Trivy server. Only
applicable in ClientServer mode.

serverUser.description: serverUser this param is the server user to be used to download db from private registry

storageClassName.description: storageClassName is the name of the storage class to be used for trivy server PVC. If empty, tries to find default storage class

storageSize.description: storageSize is the size of the trivy server PVC

configAuditReportsPlugin.description: configAuditReportsPlugin the name of the plugin that generates config audit reports.

scanJobsInSameNamespace.description: scanJobsInSameNamespace control whether to run vulnerability scan jobs in same namespace of workload

vulnerabilityReportsPlugin.description: vulnerabilityReportsPlugin the name of the plugin that generates vulnerability reports `Trivy`

storageClassName.description: storageClassName for the PVC (optional, uses cluster default if not specified)

alternateReportStorage.description: alternateReportStorage is the flag to enable alternate storage for all trivy reports (crds) in the form json files inside of a persistent volume

storageClassName.description: storageClassName for the PVC (optional, uses cluster default if not specified)

automountServiceAccountToken.description: automountServiceAccountToken the flag to enable automount for service account token

cron.description: cron this flag control the cron interval for compliance report generation

reportType.description: reportType this flag control the type of report generated (summary or all)

cron.description: cron this flag control the cron interval for compliance report generation

failEntriesLimit.description:
failEntriesLimit the flag to limit the number of fail entries per control check in the cluster compliance detail report
this limit is for preventing the report from being too large per control checks

reportType.description: reportType this flag control the type of report generated (summary or all)

specs.description:
specs is a list of compliance specs to be used by the cluster compliance scanner
- k8s-cis-1.23
- k8s-nsa-1.0
- k8s-pss-baseline-0.1
- k8s-pss-restricted-0.1
- eks-cis-1.4
- rke2-cis-1.24

excludeNamespaces.description:
excludeNamespaces is a comma separated list of namespaces (or glob patterns)
to be excluded from scanning. Only applicable in the all namespaces install
mode, i.e. when the targetNamespaces values is a blank string.

extraEnv.description: extraEnv is a list of extra environment variables for the trivy-operator.

global.description:
global values provide a centralized configuration for 'image.registry', reducing the potential for errors.
If left blank, the chart will default to the individually set 'image.registry' values

tag.description:
tag is an override of the image tag, which is by default set by the
appVersion field in Chart.yaml.

managedBy.description: managedBy is similar to .Release.Service but allows to overwrite the value

excludeNodes.description: excludeNodes comma-separated node labels that the node-collector job should exclude from scanning (example kubernetes.io/arch=arm64,team=dev)

imagePullSecret.description:
imagePullSecret is the secret name to be used when pulling node-collector image from private registries example : reg-secret
It is the user responsibility to create the secret for the private registry in `trivy-operator` namespace

tag.description: tag version of the node-collector image

tolerations.description: tolerations to be applied to the node-collector so that they can run on nodes with matching taints

useNodeSelector.description: useNodeSelector determine if to use nodeSelector (by auto detecting node name) with node-collector scan job

volumeMounts.description: node-collector pod volume mounts definition for collecting config files information

volumes.description: node-collector pod volumes definition for collecting config files information

leaderElectionId.description:
leaderElectionId determines the name of the resource that leader election
will use for holding the leader lock.

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

serverAdditionalAnnotations.description: serverAdditionalAnnotations the flag to set additional annotations for the trivy server pod

accessGlobalSecretsAndServiceAccount.description: accessGlobalSecretsAndServiceAccount The flag to enable access to global secrets/service accounts to allow `vulnerability scan job` to pull images from private registries

annotations.description: additional annotations for the operator deployment

batchDeleteDelay.description: batchDeleteDelay the duration to wait before deleting another batch of config audit reports.

batchDeleteLimit.description: batchDeleteLimit the maximum number of config audit reports deleted by the operator when the plugin's config has changed.

builtInServerRegistryInsecure.description: builtInServerRegistryInsecure is the flag to enable insecure connection from the built-in Trivy server to the registry.

builtInTrivyServer.description:
builtInTrivyServer The flag enables the usage of built-in trivy server in cluster. It also overrides the following trivy params with built-in values
trivy.mode = ClientServer and serverURL = http://<serverServiceName>.<trivy operator namespace>:4975

cacheReportTTL.description: cacheReportTTL the flag to set how long a cluster sbom report should exist. "" means that the cacheReportTTL feature is disabled

clusterComplianceEnabled.description: clusterComplianceEnabled the flag to enable cluster compliance scanner

clusterSbomCacheEnabled.description: the flag to enable cluster sbom cache generation

configAuditScannerEnabled.description: configAuditScannerEnabled the flag to enable configuration audit scanner

configAuditScannerScanOnlyCurrentRevisio.description: configAuditScannerScanOnlyCurrentRevisions the flag to only create config audit scans on the current revision of a deployment.

controllerCacheSyncTimeout.description: controllerCacheSyncTimeout the duration to wait for controller resources cache sync (default: 5m).

exposedSecretScannerEnabled.description: exposedSecretScannerEnabled the flag to enable exposed secret scanner

httpProxy.description: httpProxy is the HTTP proxy used by Trivy operator to download the default policies from GitHub.

httpsProxy.description: httpsProxy is the HTTPS proxy used by Trivy operator to download the default policies from GitHub.

infraAssessmentScannerEnabled.description: infraAssessmentScannerEnabled the flag to enable infra assessment scanner

labels.description: additional labels for the operator deployment

leaderElectionId.description:
leaderElectionId determines the name of the resource that leader election
will use for holding the leader lock.

logDevMode.description: logDevMode the flag to enable development mode (more human-readable output, extra stack traces and logging information, etc)

mergeRbacFindingWithConfigAudit.description: mergeRbacFindingWithConfigAudit the flag to enable merging rbac finding with config-audit report

metricsClusterComplianceInfo.description:
MetricsClusterComplianceInfo the flag to enable metrics about Cluster Compliance
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsConfigAuditInfo.description:
MetricsConfigAuditInfo the flag to enable metrics about configuration audits
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsExposedSecretInfo.description:
MetricsExposedSecretInfo the flag to enable metrics about exposed secrets
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsFindingsEnabled.description: metricsFindingsEnabled the flag to enable metrics for findings

metricsImageInfo.description:
MetricsImageInfo the flag to enable metrics about Image Information of scanned images
This information has image os information including os family, name/version, and if end of service life has been reached
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsInfraAssessmentInfo.description:
MetricsInfraAssessmentInfo the flag to enable metrics about Infra Assessment
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsRbacAssessmentInfo.description:
MetricsRbacAssessmentInfo the flag to enable metrics about Rbac Assessment
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

metricsVulnIdEnabled.description:
metricsVulnIdEnabled the flag to enable metrics about cve vulns id
be aware of metrics cardinality is significantly increased with this feature enabled.

namespace.description: namespace to install the operator, defaults to the .Release.Namespace

noProxy.description: noProxy is a comma separated list of IPs and domain names that are not subject to proxy settings.

podLabels.description: additional labels for the operator pod

pprofBindAddress.description: pprofBindAddress the address to bind the pprof server to. By default, it is not enabled.

privateRegistryScanSecretsNames.description: privateRegistryScanSecretsNames is map of namespace:secrets, secrets are comma seperated which can be used to authenticate in private registries in case if there no imagePullSecrets provided example : {"mynamespace":"mySecrets,anotherSecret"}

rbacAssessmentScannerEnabled.description: rbacAssessmentScannerEnabled the flag to enable rbac assessment scanner

replicas.description: replicas the number of replicas of the operator's pod

revisionHistoryLimit.description: number of old history to retain to allow rollback (if not set, default Kubernetes value is set to 10)

sbomGenerationEnabled.description: the flag to enable sbom generation, required for enabling ClusterVulnerabilityReports

scanJobTTL.description: scanJobTTL the set automatic cleanup time after the job is completed

scanJobTimeout.description: scanJobTimeout the length of time to wait before giving up on a scan job

scanJobsConcurrentLimit.description: scanJobsConcurrentLimit the maximum number of scan jobs create by the operator

scanJobsRetryDelay.description: scanJobsRetryDelay the duration to wait before retrying a failed scan job

scanNodeCollectorLimit.description: scanNodeCollectorLimit the maximum number of node collector jobs create by the operator

scanSecretTTL.description: scanSecretTTL set an automatic cleanup for scan job secrets

scannerReportTTL.description: scannerReportTTL the flag to set how long a report should exist. "" means that the ScannerReportTTL feature is disabled

serverAdditionalAnnotations.description: serverAdditionalAnnotations the flag to set additional annotations for the trivy server pod

trivyServerHealthCheckCacheExpiration.description: trivyServerHealthCheckCacheExpiration The flag to set the interval for trivy server health cache before it invalidate

valuesFromConfigMap.description: vaulesFromConfigMap name of a ConfigMap to apply OPERATOR_* environment variables. Will override Helm values.

valuesFromSecret.description: valuesFromSecret name of a Secret to apply OPERATOR_* environment variables. Will override Helm AND ConfigMap values.

vulnerabilityScannerEnabled.description: the flag to enable vulnerability scanner

vulnerabilityScannerScanOnlyCurrentRevis.description: vulnerabilityScannerScanOnlyCurrentRevisions the flag to only create vulnerability scans on the current revision of a deployment.

webhookBroadcastCustomHeaders.description: webhookBroadcastCustomHeaders the flag to set webhook endpoint sent with custom defined headers if webhookBroadcastURL is enabled

webhookBroadcastTimeout.description: webhookBroadcastTimeout the flag to set timeout for webhook requests if webhookBroadcastURL is enabled

webhookBroadcastURL.description: webhookBroadcastURL the flag to set reports should be sent to a webhook endpoint. "" means that the webhookBroadcastURL feature is disabled

webhookSendDeletedReports.description: webhookSendDeletedReports the flag to enable sending deleted reports if webhookBroadcastURL is enabled

podAnnotations.description: podAnnotations annotations added to the operator's pod

registryPassword.description: registryPassword is the password for the registry

registryUser.description: registryUser is the user for the registry

existingSecret.description:
existingSecret if a secret containing registry credentials that have been created outside the chart (e.g external-secrets, sops, etc...).
Keys must be at least one of the following: policies.bundle.oci.user, policies.bundle.oci.password
Overrides policiesBundle.registryUser, policiesBundle.registryPassword values.
Note: The secret has to be named "trivy-operator".

insecure.description: insecure is the flag to enable insecure connection to the policy bundle registry

registryPassword.description: registryPassword is the password for the registry

registryUser.description: registryUser is the user for the registry

tag.description: tag version of the policies bundle

service.description:
service only expose a metrics endpoint for prometheus to scrape,
trivy-operator does not have a user interface.

annotations.description: annotations added to the operator's service

headless.description: if true, the Service doesn't allocate any IP

nodePort.description: the nodeport to use when service type is LoadBalancer or NodePort. If not set, Kubernetes automatically select one.

name.description:
name specifies the name of the k8s Service Account. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

name specifies the name of the k8s Service Account. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created.

name.description:
name specifies the name of the k8s Service Account. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

endpointAdditionalProperties.description: EndpointAdditionalProperties allows setting additional properties on the endpoint such as relabelings, metricRelabelings etc.

authorization.description:
authorization configures the Authorization header credentials used by
the client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `basicAuth`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

credentials.description: credentials defines a key of a Secret in the namespace that contains the credentials for authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

type.description:
type defines the authentication type. The value is case-insensitive.

"Basic" is not a supported value.

Default: "Bearer"

basicAuth.description:
basicAuth defines the Basic Authentication credentials used by the
client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

password.description:
password defines a key of a Secret containing the password for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

username.description:
username defines a key of a Secret containing the username for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

bearerTokenFile.description:
bearerTokenFile defines the file to read bearer token for scraping the target.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

bearerTokenSecret.description:
bearerTokenSecret defines a key of a Secret containing the bearer token
used by the client for authentication. The secret needs to be in the
same namespace as the custom resource and readable by the Prometheus
Operator.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `oauth2`.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

enableHttp2.description: enableHttp2 can be used to disable HTTP2.

filterRunning.description:
filterRunning when true, the pods which are not running (e.g. either in Failed or
Succeeded state) are dropped during the target discovery.

If unset, the filtering is enabled.

More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#pod-phase

followRedirects.description:
followRedirects defines whether the client should follow HTTP 3xx
redirects.

honorLabels.description:
honorLabels defines when true the metric's labels when they collide
with the target's labels.

honorTimestamps.description:
honorTimestamps defines whether Prometheus preserves the timestamps
when exposed by the target.

interval.description:
interval at which Prometheus scrapes the metrics from the target.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape interval.

metricRelabelings.description:
metricRelabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply to the
samples before ingestion.

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

noProxy.description:
noProxy defines a comma-separated string that can contain IPs, CIDR notation, domain names
that should be excluded from proxying. IP and domain names can
contain port numbers.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

oauth2.description:
oauth2 defines the OAuth2 settings used by the client.

It requires Prometheus >= 2.27.0.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `bearerTokenSecret`.

clientId.description:
clientId defines a key of a Secret or ConfigMap containing the
OAuth2 client's ID.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key to select.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

secret.description: secret defines the Secret containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

clientSecret.description:
clientSecret defines a key of a Secret containing the OAuth2
client's secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

endpointParams.description:
endpointParams configures the HTTP parameters to append to the token
URL.

noProxy.description:
noProxy defines a comma-separated string that can contain IPs, CIDR notation, domain names
that should be excluded from proxying. IP and domain names can
contain port numbers.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

items.description: SecretKeySelector selects a key of a Secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

proxyConnectHeader.description:
proxyConnectHeader optionally specifies headers to send to
proxies during CONNECT requests.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyFromEnvironment.description:
proxyFromEnvironment defines whether to use the proxy configuration defined by environment variables (HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY).

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyUrl.description: proxyUrl defines the HTTP proxy server to use.

scopes.description: scopes defines the OAuth2 scopes used for the token request.

tlsConfig.description:
tlsConfig defines the TLS configuration to use when connecting to the OAuth2 server.
It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0.

ca.description: ca defines the Certificate authority used when verifying server certificates.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key to select.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

minVersion.description:

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

tokenUrl.description: tokenUrl defines the URL to fetch the token from.

params.description: params define optional HTTP URL parameters.

path.description:
path defines the HTTP path from which to scrape for metrics.

If empty, Prometheus uses the default value (e.g. `/metrics`).

port.description:
port defines the name of the Service port which this endpoint refers to.

It takes precedence over `targetPort`.

proxyConnectHeader.description:
proxyConnectHeader optionally specifies headers to send to
proxies during CONNECT requests.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyFromEnvironment.description:
proxyFromEnvironment defines whether to use the proxy configuration defined by environment variables (HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY).

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyUrl.description: proxyUrl defines the HTTP proxy server to use.

relabelings.description:
relabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply the target's
metadata labels.

The Operator automatically adds relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields.

The original scrape job's name is available via the `__tmp_prometheus_job_name` label.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

scheme.description: scheme defines the HTTP scheme to use when scraping the metrics.

scrapeTimeout.description:
scrapeTimeout defines the timeout after which Prometheus considers the scrape to be failed.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape timeout unless it is less
than the target's scrape interval value in which the latter is used.
The value cannot be greater than the scrape interval otherwise the operator will reject the resource.

targetPort.description:
targetPort defines the name or number of the target port of the `Pod` object behind the
Service. The port must be specified with the container's port property.

tlsConfig.description: tlsConfig defines TLS configuration used by the client.

ca.description: ca defines the Certificate authority used when verifying server certificates.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key to select.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

certFile.description: certFile defines the path to the client cert file in the Prometheus container for the targets.

insecureSkipVerify.description: insecureSkipVerify defines how to disable target certificate validation.

keyFile.description: keyFile defines the path to the client key file in the Prometheus container for the targets.

keySecret.description: keySecret defines the Secret containing the client key file for the targets.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

maxVersion.description:
maxVersion defines the maximum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.41.0 or Thanos >= v0.31.0.

minVersion.description:
minVersion defines the minimum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.35.0 or Thanos >= v0.28.0.

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

trackTimestampsStaleness.description:
trackTimestampsStaleness defines whether Prometheus tracks staleness of
the metrics that have an explicit timestamp present in scraped data.
Has no effect if `honorTimestamps` is false.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.48.0.

honorLabels.description: HonorLabels chooses the metric’s labels on collisions with target labels

interval.description: Interval at which metrics should be scraped. If not specified Prometheus’ global scrape interval is used.

namespace.description: The namespace where Prometheus expects to find service monitors

serviceMonitor.description:
Prometheus ServiceMonitor configuration -- to install the trivy operator with the ServiceMonitor
you must have Prometheus already installed and running. If you do not have Prometheus installed, enabling this will
produce an error.

enabled.description: enabled determines whether a serviceMonitor should be deployed

endpointAdditionalProperties.description: EndpointAdditionalProperties allows setting additional properties on the endpoint such as relabelings, metricRelabelings etc.

authorization.description:
authorization configures the Authorization header credentials used by
the client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `basicAuth`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

credentials.description: credentials defines a key of a Secret in the namespace that contains the credentials for authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

type.description:
type defines the authentication type. The value is case-insensitive.

"Basic" is not a supported value.

Default: "Bearer"

basicAuth.description:
basicAuth defines the Basic Authentication credentials used by the
client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

password.description:
password defines a key of a Secret containing the password for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

username.description:
username defines a key of a Secret containing the username for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

bearerTokenFile.description:
bearerTokenFile defines the file to read bearer token for scraping the target.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

bearerTokenSecret.description:
bearerTokenSecret defines a key of a Secret containing the bearer token
used by the client for authentication. The secret needs to be in the
same namespace as the custom resource and readable by the Prometheus
Operator.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `oauth2`.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

enableHttp2.description: enableHttp2 can be used to disable HTTP2.

filterRunning.description:
filterRunning when true, the pods which are not running (e.g. either in Failed or
Succeeded state) are dropped during the target discovery.

If unset, the filtering is enabled.

More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#pod-phase

followRedirects.description:
followRedirects defines whether the client should follow HTTP 3xx
redirects.

honorLabels.description:
honorLabels defines when true the metric's labels when they collide
with the target's labels.

honorTimestamps.description:
honorTimestamps defines whether Prometheus preserves the timestamps
when exposed by the target.

interval.description:
interval at which Prometheus scrapes the metrics from the target.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape interval.

metricRelabelings.description:
metricRelabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply to the
samples before ingestion.

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

noProxy.description:
noProxy defines a comma-separated string that can contain IPs, CIDR notation, domain names
that should be excluded from proxying. IP and domain names can
contain port numbers.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

oauth2.description:
oauth2 defines the OAuth2 settings used by the client.

It requires Prometheus >= 2.27.0.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `bearerTokenSecret`.

clientId.description:
clientId defines a key of a Secret or ConfigMap containing the
OAuth2 client's ID.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key to select.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:

This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

clientSecret.description:
clientSecret defines a key of a Secret containing the OAuth2
client's secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

endpointParams.description:
endpointParams configures the HTTP parameters to append to the token
URL.

noProxy.description:
noProxy defines a comma-separated string that can contain IPs, CIDR notation, domain names
that should be excluded from proxying. IP and domain names can
contain port numbers.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyFromEnvironment.description:
proxyFromEnvironment defines whether to use the proxy configuration defined by environment variables (HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY).

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyUrl.description: proxyUrl defines the HTTP proxy server to use.

scopes.description: scopes defines the OAuth2 scopes used for the token request.

tlsConfig.description:

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0.

Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

maxVersion.description:
maxVersion defines the maximum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.41.0 or Thanos >= v0.31.0.

minVersion.description:
minVersion defines the minimum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.35.0 or Thanos >= v0.28.0.

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

trackTimestampsStaleness.description:
trackTimestampsStaleness defines whether Prometheus tracks staleness of
the metrics that have an explicit timestamp present in scraped data.
Has no effect if `honorTimestamps` is false.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.48.0.

honorLabels.description: HonorLabels chooses the metric’s labels on collisions with target labels

interval.description: Interval at which metrics should be scraped. If not specified Prometheus’ global scrape interval is used.

namespace.description: The namespace where Prometheus expects to find service monitors

targetNamespaces.description:
targetNamespace defines where you want trivy-operator to operate. By
default, it's a blank string to select all namespaces, but you can specify
another namespace, or a comma separated list of namespaces.

targetWorkloads.description:
targetWorkloads is a comma seperated list of Kubernetes workload resources
to be included in the vulnerability and config-audit scans
if left blank, all workload resources will be scanned

githubToken.description:
githubToken is the GitHub access token used by Trivy to download the vulnerabilities
database from GitHub. Only applicable in Standalone mode.

additionalVulnerabilityReportFields.description:
additionalVulnerabilityReportFields is a comma separated list of additional fields which
can be added to the VulnerabilityReport. Supported parameters: Description, Links, CVSS, Target, Class, PackagePath and PackageType

clientServerSkipUpdate.description:
clientServerSkipUpdate is the flag to enable skip databases update for Trivy client.
Only applicable in ClientServer mode.

command.description:
command. One of `image`, `filesystem` or `rootfs` scanning, depending on the target type required for the scan.
For 'filesystem' and `rootfs` scanning, ensure that the `trivyOperator.scanJobPodTemplateContainerSecurityContext` is configured
to run as the root user (runAsUser = 0).

configFile.description:
configFile can be used to tell Trivy to use specific options available only in the config file (e.g. Mirror registries).
configFile:
registry:
mirrors:
index.docker.io:
- mirror.with.bad.auth
- mirror.without.image

createConfig.description: createConfig indicates whether to create config objects

dbRepositoryInsecure.description: The Flag to enable insecure connection for downloading trivy-db via proxy (air-gaped env)

debug.description: debug One of `true` or `false`. Enables debug mode.

existingSecret.description:
existingSecret if a secret containing gitHubToken, serverToken or serverCustomHeaders has been created outside the chart (e.g external-secrets, sops, etc...).
Keys must be at least one of the following: trivy.githubToken, trivy.serverToken, trivy.serverCustomHeaders
Overrides trivy.gitHubToken, trivy.serverToken, trivy.serverCustomHeaders values.
Note: The secret has to be named "trivy-operator-trivy-config".

externalRegoPoliciesEnabled.description: The Flag to enable the usage of external rego policies config-map, this should be used when the user wants to use their own rego policies

filesystemScanCacheDir.description:
filesystemScanCacheDir the flag to set custom path for trivy filesystem scan `cache-dir` parameter.
Only applicable in filesystem scan mode.

githubToken.description:
githubToken is the GitHub access token used by Trivy to download the vulnerabilities
database from GitHub. Only applicable in Standalone mode.

httpProxy.description: httpProxy is the HTTP proxy used by Trivy to download the vulnerabilities database from GitHub.

httpsProxy.description: httpsProxy is the HTTPS proxy used by Trivy to download the vulnerabilities database from GitHub.

ignoreFile.description:
ignoreFile can be used to tell Trivy to ignore vulnerabilities by ID (one per line)
ignoreFile:
- CVE-1970-0001
- CVE-1970-0002

ignoreFileName.description:
ignoreFileName sets the file name that will be mounted inside the scanner container.
Defaults to ".trivyignore" if not set.

ignoreUnfixed.description:
ignoreUnfixed is the flag to show only fixed vulnerabilities in
vulnerabilities reported by Trivy. Set to true to enable it.

imagePullSecret.description:
imagePullSecret is the secret name to be used when pulling trivy image from private registries example : reg-secret
It is the user responsibility to create the secret for the private registry in `trivy-operator` namespace

pullPolicy.description: pullPolicy is the imge pull policy used for trivy image , valid values are (Always, Never, IfNotPresent)

tag.description: tag version of the Trivy image

imageScanCacheDir.description:
imageScanCacheDir the flag to set custom path for trivy image scan `cache-dir` parameter.
Only applicable in image scan mode.

includeDevDeps.description:
includeDevDeps include development dependencies in the report (supported: npm, yarn) (default: false)
note: this flag is only applicable when trivy.command is set to filesystem

insecureRegistries.description:
The registry to which insecure connections are allowed. There can be multiple registries with different keys.
pocRegistry: poc.myregistry.harbor.com.pl
qaRegistry: qa.registry.aquasec.com
internalRegistry: registry.registry.svc:5000

javaDbRegistry.description: javaDbRegistry is the registry for the Java vulnerability database.

labels.description: labels is the extra labels to be used for trivy server statefulset

mode.description:
mode is the Trivy client mode. Either Standalone or ClientServer. Depending
on the active mode other settings might be applicable or required.

noProxy.description: noProxy is a comma separated list of IPs and domain names that are not subject to proxy settings.

nonSslRegistries.description:
Registries without SSL. There can be multiple registries with different keys.
pocRegistry: poc.myregistry.harbor.com.pl
qaRegistry: qa.registry.aquasec.com
internalRegistry: registry.registry.svc:5000

offlineScan.description:
offlineScan is the flag to enable the offline scan functionality in Trivy
This will prevent outgoing HTTP requests, e.g. to search.maven.org

podLabels.description: podLabels is the extra pod labels to be used for trivy server

priorityClassName.description: priorityClassName is the name of the priority class used for trivy server

registry.description:
Mirrored registries. There can be multiple registries with different keys.
Make sure to quote registries containing dots

resources.description: resources resource requests and limits for scan job containers

sbomSources.description: sbomSources trivy will try to retrieve SBOM from the specified sources (oci,rekor)

volumes.description:
- name: trusted-ca
mountPath: /etc/ssl/trusted-ca
subPath: trusted-ca.crt
readOnly: true
Statefulset volumes
- name: trusted-ca
secret:
secretName: trusted-ca

replicas.description: the number of replicas of the trivy-server

serverCustomHeaders.description:
serverCustomHeaders is a comma separated list of custom HTTP headers sent by
Trivy client to Trivy server. Only applicable in ClientServer mode.
serverCustomHeaders: "foo=bar"

serverInsecure.description: serverInsecure is the flag to enable insecure connection to the Trivy server.

serverPassword.description: serverPassword this param is the server user to be used to download db from private registry

serverServiceName.description: serverServiceName this param is the server service name to be used in cluster

serverToken.description:
serverToken is the token to authenticate Trivy client with Trivy server. Only
applicable in ClientServer mode.

serverTokenHeader.description:
serverTokenHeader is the name of the HTTP header used to send the authentication
token to Trivy server. Only application in ClientServer mode when
trivy.serverToken is specified.

serverUser.description: serverUser this param is the server user to be used to download db from private registry

severity.description: severity is a comma separated list of severity levels reported by Trivy.

skipDirs.description: a comma separated list of directories for Trivy to skip

skipFiles.description: a comma separated list of file paths for Trivy to skip

skipJavaDBUpdate.description: skipJavaDBUpdate is the flag to enable skip Java index databases update for Trivy client.

slow.description: slow this flag is to use less CPU/memory for scanning though it takes more time than normal scanning. It fits small-footprint

sslCertDir.description: sslCertDir can be used to override the system default locations for SSL certificate files directory, example: /ssl/certs

storageClassEnabled.description: whether to use a storage class for trivy server or emptydir (one mey want to use ephemeral storage)

storageClassName.description: storageClassName is the name of the storage class to be used for trivy server PVC. If empty, tries to find default storage class

storageSize.description: storageSize is the size of the trivy server PVC

supportedConfigAuditKinds.description: The Flag is the list of supported kinds separated by comma delimiter to be scanned by the config audit scanner

timeout.description: timeout is the duration to wait for scan completion.

useBuiltinRegoPolicies.description: The Flag to enable the usage of builtin rego policies by default, these policies are downloaded by default from mirror.gcr.io/aquasec/trivy-checks

useEmbeddedRegoPolicies.description:
To enable the usage of embedded rego policies, set the flag useEmbeddedRegoPolicies. This should serve as a fallback for air-gapped environments.
When useEmbeddedRegoPolicies is set to true, useBuiltinRegoPolicies should be set to false.

valuesFromConfigMap.description: vaulesFromConfigMap name of a ConfigMap to apply TRIVY_* environment variables. Will override Helm values.

valuesFromSecret.description: valuesFromSecret name of a Secret to apply TRIVY_* environment variables. Will override Helm AND ConfigMap values.

vulnType.description: vulnType can be used to tell Trivy to filter vulnerabilities by a pkg-type (library, os)

additionalReportLabels.description:
additionalReportLabels comma-separated representation of the labels which the user wants the scanner pods to be
labeled with. Example: `foo=bar,env=stage` will labeled the reports with the labels `foo: bar` and `env: stage`

configAuditReportsPlugin.description: configAuditReportsPlugin the name of the plugin that generates config audit reports.

excludeImages.description:
excludeImages is comma separated glob patterns for excluding images from scanning.
Example: pattern: `k8s.gcr.io/*/*` will exclude image: `k8s.gcr.io/coredns/coredns:v1.8.0`.

metricsResourceLabelsPrefix.description:
metricsResourceLabelsPrefix Prefix that will be prepended to the labels names indicated in `reportResourceLabels`
when including them in the Prometheus metrics

policiesConfig.description:
policiesConfig Custom Rego Policies to be used by the config audit scanner
See https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/blob/main/docs/tutorials/writing-custom-configuration-audit-policies.md for more details.

reportRecordFailedChecksOnly.description: reportRecordFailedChecksOnly flag is to record only failed checks on misconfiguration reports (config-audit and rbac assessment)

reportResourceLabels.description:
reportResourceLabels comma-separated scanned resource labels which the user wants to include in the Prometheus
metrics report. Example: `owner,app`

scanJobAffinity.description: scanJobAffinity affinity to be applied to the scanner pods and node-collector

scanJobAnnotations.description:
scanJobAnnotations comma-separated representation of the annotations which the user wants the scanner jobs and pods to be
annotated with. Example: `foo=bar,env=stage` will annotate the scanner jobs and pods with the annotations `foo: bar` and `env: stage`

scanJobAutomountServiceAccountToken.description: scanJobAutomountServiceAccountToken the flag to enable automount for service account token on scan job

scanJobCompressLogs.description: scanJobCompressLogs control whether scanjob output should be compressed or plain

scanJobCustomVolumes.description:
scanJobCustomVolumes add custom volumes to the scan job
- name: var-lib-etcd
hostPath:
path: /var/lib/etcd

scanJobCustomVolumesMount.description:
scanJobCustomVolumesMount add custom volumes mount to the scan job
- name: var-lib-etcd
mountPath: /var/lib/etcd
readOnly: true

scanJobNodeSelector.description: scanJobNodeSelector nodeSelector to be applied to the scanner pods so that they can run on nodes with matching labels

scanJobPodPriorityClassName.description: scanJobPodPriorityClassName Priority class name to be set on the pods created by trivy operator jobs. This accepts a string value

scanJobPodTemplateContainerSecurityConte.description:
scanJobPodTemplateContainerSecurityContext SecurityContext the user wants the scanner and node collector containers (and their
initContainers) to be amended with.

scanJobPodTemplateLabels.description:
scanJobPodTemplateLabels comma-separated representation of the labels which the user wants the scanner pods to be
labeled with. Example: `foo=bar,env=stage` will labeled the scanner pods with the labels `foo: bar` and `env: stage`

scanJobPodTemplatePodSecurityContext.description:
scanJobPodTemplatePodSecurityContext podSecurityContext the user wants the scanner and node collector pods to be amended with.
Example:
RunAsUser: 10000
RunAsGroup: 10000
RunAsNonRoot: true

scanJobTolerations.description: scanJobTolerations tolerations to be applied to the scanner pods so that they can run on nodes with matching taints

scanJobsInSameNamespace.description: scanJobsInSameNamespace control whether to run vulnerability scan jobs in same namespace of workload

skipInitContainers.description: skipInitContainers when this flag is set to true, the initContainers will be skipped for the scanner and node collector pods

skipResourceByLabels.description: skipResourceByLabels comma-separated labels keys which trivy-operator will skip scanning on resources with matching labels

useGCRServiceAccount.description: useGCRServiceAccount the flag to enable the usage of GCR service account for scanning images in GCR

vulnerabilityReportsPlugin.description: vulnerabilityReportsPlugin the name of the plugin that generates vulnerability reports `Trivy`
